---
title: "Runtime Adapters, Tool Permissions, and Human Approval"
chapter_id: "runtime-adapters-tool-permissions-and-human-approval"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "talos"
  - "viea"
  - "ladon_manhattan"
  - "software_magic_grimoire"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Runtime Adapters, Tool Permissions, and Human Approval

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `runtime-adapters-tool-permissions-and-human-approval` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `talos`, `viea`, `ladon_manhattan`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `genesiscode`, `moecot` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Plans become real-world effects only through tools, runtimes, deployment adapters, and approval gates.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Tool calling without a permission and approval model turns reasoning errors into side effects.

## Core claim

[runtime-adapters-tool-permissions-and-human-approval.core, support: argument] Runtime adapters should enforce typed permissions, sandboxing, human approval, and post-action evidence capture.

## Mechanism

- Map jobs to runtime adapters with declared capabilities.
- Require permission checks and approval gates for high-impact actions.
- Record deployment evidence and rollback handles.

## Interfaces

- Execution owns adapters.
- Security mediates secrets.
- Evidence records deployment outcomes.

## Invariants

- No adapter executes beyond granted scope.
- High-impact actions require approval.
- Rollback handles are captured when available.

## Failure modes

- Tool overreach.
- Approval bypass.
- Irreversible deployment without rollback.

## Minimal implementation

A runtime-adapter contract with permissions, approval requirements, and result evidence.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Adapter permission test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Approval gate enforcement test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Rollback handle capture test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `ladon_manhattan` | Ladon & The Manhattan Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Kernel-level security architecture for high-agency AI. |
| `software_magic_grimoire` | Software Magic Grimoire | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Structured command contracts and semantic density of software words. |
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
